observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant – Designate/
Municipal Engineer admitted that
inventory was not yet made on PPE.
Not conducted on municipal road
networks.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer
answered that inventory was not yet
made on PPE.
A SCABAA was only submitted late.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer
mentioned that the said DVs were
already submitted.

Management admitted on the absence
of TCT for the municipal lots.

Management agreed to establish RPT
and SET Receivables for the succeeding
years.

Management agreed to comply with the
recommendation. Succeeding
transactions should be properly
supported with complete documentation
in order to establish the validity of
claims.

A CLUP was already prepared and
approved on January 28, 2020. Copy of
the final plan shall be verified due to
non-submission of the approved copy of
said plan. Hence, the management is
recommended to submit the same
immediately.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer
commented that he will look into these
contracts. No additional documents were
submitted to the auditor.

The Municipal Engineer admitted that
only the invitation is being posted in the
PhilGeps and agreed to comply on the
posting requirements for CY 2018
procurement. Verification in CY 2019 the
PhilGeps website showed a number of
invitation to bid by the LGU but no Notice
of Award was published. Moreover, list
of projects completed for the year and on-
going at year-end was not submitted as
reiterated in Observation No. 20 of this
report.

Management agreed to comply with the
recommendation.

Management agreed to submit the
documents in coordination with the DSWD-
KALAHI since the funded projects were
already not completed.

Management agreed to comply on the
recommendations.

Management claimed that a GFPS was
established and agreed to comply on the
other recommendation. As of this report
date, none was submitted to the audit
team. There was no approval by the LGU
officials.

Management commented that there was
no change on the status of the
suspensions and disallowances. The
management exerts more effort on the
settlement of the suspensions and
disallowances.

For CY 2018, the Acting Municipal
Treasurer implemented. There was no
submission for CY 2017 transactions.
There was no submission of required
documents. Reiterated in Observation
No.32 of this report.

For CY 2018, the DPWH prepared ABC
for contracts issued by the DPWH. Copies
of bidding documents for CY 2018 were
not submitted including the contracts.
No documents were submitted.

Not implemented. Management agreed
to comply on the recommendation.
No additional documents were
submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended that the:
1.5.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct a
yearly inventory taking and to prepare and submit the Report on
Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team and
Accounting Office; and
1.5.2 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as the
General Services Officer reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of
the LGU and verify variances for subsequent adjustments as
necessary and maintain subsidiary and property ledger cards for
PPE.

2.10 We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
2.10.1 The Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
2.10.2 The Municipal Accountant to take up in the books upon
verification as to the status of the road networks completed in CY
2015 and 2014 totaling ₱13,466,149.73; trace the recorded road,
highways and bridges which were closed to government equity
from 2013 and prior; keep and maintain Registry of Public
Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records for roads and
road components for every road network; and
2.10.3 The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects.

3.6 We have recommended for management to determine the details of the
recorded Land in the books and exhaust all possible means to document
the legality of ownership of the lots through Original Certificate of Title
or Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the LGU.

5.5 We have recommended that the:
5.5.1 Municipal Accountant establish the correct amount of collectible
as of year-end supported with certified list of taxpayers;
5.5.2 Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of taxpayers and
submit to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in setting up the
receivables and deferred income in order to present fairly the
financial statements; and
5.5.3 Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the
updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate
in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter.

6.11 We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
6.11.1 issue demand letters to the accountable persons with unliquidated
cash advances;
6.11.2 henceforth, enforce the full liquidation of all cash advances after
the purpose had been served and demand the immediate refund of
the excess cash advance upon submission of the Liquidation
Report pursuant to the provision of COA Circular 97-002; and
6.11.3 require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional
cash advances are granted to any official unless the previous cash
advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made and
to strictly monitor unsettled cash advances of each officers and
employees and send reminders within ten (10) days before
expiration of the prescribed period.

4.13 We have recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:
4.13.1 deposit immediately the accumulated collections amounting to
₱237,605.61;
4.13.2 require the Revenue Collecting Clerks to remit their collections
daily together with report of collections and remittance and
duplicate copies of receipts;
4.13.3 henceforth, deposit all collections regularly and intact pursuant to
above-mentioned rules and regulations.

5.13 We have recommended that:
5.13.1 the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace back
the source of the abnormal balances relating to transactions in the
previous years, reconcile their records for the year 2018
specifically the months mentioned in Paragraph 1.6 and the record
the verified adjustments;
5.13.2 the Municipal Accountant:
submit the Official Receipts pertaining to the remittances
under paragraph 5.4
maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for all
payables to avoid over and under remittances
and reconcile her books with the books of these agencies
regularly;
5.13.3 Henceforth, management ensure that all trust collections are
promptly remitted to the concerned agencies to avoid
imposition of penalties and inconveniences to employee-members.

7.8 We have recommended that:
7.8.1 Management justify the noted lapses and submit the required
reports as basis for the preparation of the Statement of
Comparison of the Budget and Actual Amounts.
7.8.2 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all Obligation
Requests are duly accomplished; and
7.8.3 That the Municipal Budget Officer maintains and prepares the
required registries and Statement of Appropriations, Allotments,
Obligations and Balances.

8.4 We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to provide additional
disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements for the Local Road
Networks.

12.4 We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the
documents enumerated under item 12.1 to properly evaluate the
completeness and compliance with the requirements for supplemental
feeding.

16.8 We have recommended management to justify the noted lapses and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the
above-mentioned rules and regulations.

18.5 We have recommended that:
18.5.1 Management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as
guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and
furnish COA of the same for audit purposes.
18.5.2 Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in
accordance with the approved APP of the municipality.

19.5 We have recommended management to:
19.5.1 Submit all contracts perfected in CY 2018;
19.5.2 Henceforth, submit all perfected contracts and purchase orders to
COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate
review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and
other necessary audit procedures; and
19.5.3 Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to
comply with the required submission dates of contracts and
purchase orders.

20.6 We have recommended management to consider the default mode of
procurement which is through competitive public bidding. Resort to
alternative methods of procurement such as shopping or small value
procurement shall be in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184.

21.3 We have recommended management to Annually submit accomplishment
report for the 20% development fund.

22.7 We have recommended management to:
22.7.1 Submit immediately the noted disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents in paragraph 22.3; and
22.7.2 Implement only projects set forth in the Annual Investment Plan.
In instances where other priority projects are identified for
implementation, a Sanggunian Bayan resolution should first be
passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment
Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sanggunian
Panlalawigan.

23.5 We have recommended management to strictly comply with the
provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in
the preparation and utilization of the 20% DF to attain the desired socio-
economic and environmental outcomes.

28.6 We have recommended management to utilize the appropriated annual
LDRRMF for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response
rehabilitation and recovery and in accordance with the approved
LDRRMF Investment Plan.

29.6 We have recommended for management to treat the unexpended/
unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for the current year and
unexpended LDRRMF from prior years as Trust Liability and be duly
transferred to a special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds.

30.6 We have recommended for management to provide in the Notes to
Financial Statements all the required disclosures and as mandated under
COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

32.6 We have recommended for management to:
32.6.1 maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the
implementation of GAD related PPAs in order to achieve the
objective for which funds were provided; and
32.6.2 prepare a reliable GAD accomplishment report through
accompanying the breakdown of expenditures for proper
evaluation of the capacity of the LGU to address GAD issues by
the oversight agencies.

33.4.6 We have recommended the GFPS to prepare the GPB through the
application of the HGDG to include only programs and activities
that address the impact of the gender issues and inequality.

observations.managementAction:
Management agreed to adjust the
depreciation.

Management claimed that it is
difficult to collect the unliquidated cash
advances.

Management claimed that the drafted
SWMP was already approved as of end
2019 and submitted. Only the draft
SWMP was given to the Audit team.
The audit team could not verify the
implementation of a SWMP due to non-
submission of the plan and
accomplishment report. No document
was submitted for the procured
shredder in CY 2017.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer stated
that the said DVs were already
submitted. Although the DVs were
submitted, no accomplishment report
was submitted. Likewise, for CY 2018,
none was submitted.

Management fully complied with the
creation of DRRM Office. No comment
on the VAA. Management agreed to
comply on the recommendation on the
other items. The report for CY 2018 was
not submitted as reiterated in
Observation No.27 of this report. No
report was submitted for CY 2018 as
reiterated in Observation No.27 of this
report. Management agreed to comply
with the recommendation on establishing
a special trust fund account. Management
agreed to comply on the recommended
actions.

An LCPC was already created.
Management agreed to comply on the
submission of the copy of the SB
Resolution/Executive Order for the
creation of LCPC.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer ensured
to remit the said balances to the BIR.
Management recorded the adjustment.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following:

The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and 
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: For implementation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties, the Municipal Mayor form a committee which will oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Moreover, result of the inventory should be submitted to the audit team through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.
observations.managementAction: For implementation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that copies of the debit memoranda together with the supporting documents be submitted immediately to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management should determine the details of the recorded Land in the books. Also, we have recommended that the Management exhausts all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will conduct Municipal Wide Tax mapping to determine the details of the recorded Land in the books

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish the correct amount of collectible as of year-end supported with certified list of taxpayers. The management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and submit to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in setting up the receivables and deferred income in order to present fairly the financial statements.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter.
observations.managementAction: Certified List of Taxpayers will be submitted as basis for setting up the receivables

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by regulations. Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management will ensure timely submission of financial reports and accounts

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume her previous position.
observations.managementAction: The management will hire a Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of an approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2016 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.
observations.managementAction: The APP will be submitted to COA

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following: 

That management justify the above noted lapses and submit the missing documents for evaluation; 
That henceforth, the BAC conduct post-qualification and strictly follow the “pass/fail” criteria in the conduct of bidding and automatically disqualify non-complying bidders; and 
That management strictly comply with the regulations set forth under RA 9184, its implementing rules and regulations and promulgations by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution properly authorizing the Municipality of Bucloc to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further the municipality should maintain a separate book for the said fund as well as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to account for its transactions; and
Submit the Special Allotment Release Orders and Notices of Authority to Debit Account Issued.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:
	
The management specifically identify and prepared a detail list of the priority projects to be funded under the 20% development fund which should include particular location and time frame of implementation. Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these programs or project should already been included in the planning and budgeting.
Submit the Program of Works as supporting documents to the Annual Investment Plan to aid in the further analysis of Appropriated Projects vis-a-vis actual expenditures. For lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained.
The management should strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental condition of constituents.
The management should require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all amounts allotted for the 20% development fund for easier monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will strictly observe the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM MC 2011-1

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

Justify the noted lapses and henceforth, familiarize themselves with the relevant provisions with regard to the utilization of the LDRRM Funds; 
Push for the enactment of a local ordinance creating the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Office including the required staffing/personnel. Upon enactment consider the immediate hiring of qualified DRRM Officer and staff; 
Require the MDRRMO-Designate to prepare and submit to the audit team the report of utilization of the LDRRMF. Utilization reports should be submitted on the prescribed time set forth by the COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Submit the required supporting documents needed in order to validate whether the procured goods were distributed to its intended beneficiaries.
See to it that unexpended/unobligated funds from prior years LDRRM are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust fund account; and
Utilize the prior years’ trust liability for the acquisition of disaster preparedness equipment as well as immediate assistance and services to calamity stricken constituents.
observations.managementAction: The position for the LDRRMO was included in the plantilla and already budgeted

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Also, submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2016, if any. Henceforth, observe and abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
observations.managementAction: For compliance
No action informed

observations.recommendation:
1.10	We have recommended that, in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, the management undertake the following:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain subsidiary leger for roads and its components and reconcile the same result of the physical inventory at the end of each year.

2.6	We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties, the Municipal Mayor form a committee which will oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Moreover, result of the inventory should be submitted to the audit team through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.

3.6	To present the correct balances, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to record the depreciation expenses for CY2017 as follows:
3.7	Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that depreciation expenses were provided to all applicable Property, Plant and Equipment accounts to ensure fairness of presentation in the financial statements.
4.6	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, prepare the Statement of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation and submit the same to the audit team for verification.

5.8	We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers as well as liquidation reports be submitted immediately to the Auditor for verification, otherwise, management must cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of officials concerned until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission, without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against such official or employee pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

6.6	We have recommended that management  determine the details of the recorded Land in the books and exhausts all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

7.5	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish the correct amount of collectible as of year-end supported with certified list of taxpayers. The management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and submit to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in setting up the receivables and deferred income in order to present fairly the financial statements.
7.6	We also have recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter.

8.5.5	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert effort in determining the composition of unliquidated cash advances in the previous years. Moreover, management demand from the retired MPDC the refund of payment of loan amortization amounting to ₱199,632.08. Consider withholding any retirement benefits due him which can be legally withheld.  Otherwise, this will become the personal liability of the person responsible for the remittance thereof;

9.10	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by regulations. Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation   in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.
10.9	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.

11.10	We have recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume his previous position.
12.7	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2018 onwards.

13.6	We have recommended that the BAC submit copies of duly perfected contracts within five (5) working days from perfection thereof to the Auditor for prompt review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001. Also, submit immediately to COA the contract documents of the above noted projects awarded in CY 2017 for evaluation.
14.4	We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.
15.7	We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of an approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2017 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

16.7	In view of the noted violations, we have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. Submit required documentary evidences and henceforth, all fund transfers to other LGUs should be supported by Memorandum of Agreement.

17.10	In view of the noted violations, we have recommended the following:
The Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution properly authorizing the Municipality of Bucloc to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further the municipality maintain a separate books for the said fund as well as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to account for its transactions; and
The Municipal Accountant submit copies of Notices of Authority to Debit Account Issued, as well as the approved Sangguniang Ordinance/ Resolution submitted to DBM or supplemental budget/ investment plan covering the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax

18.7	We have recommended that management:
Facilitate the completion of the 10-year solid waste management plan and submit to National Solid Waste Management Commission for review and approval;
Establish a Solid Waste Management Board that will supervise the implementation of SWMP. Henceforth, abide with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003; and
Submit necessary documents for the procurement of plastic shredder for evaluation and ensure compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 or the procurement law.

19.4.5   We have recommended management to:
19.4.5.1	Submit immediately the disbursement vouchers for the period July to December 2017 as well as the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund;
19.4.5.2	Implement only projects set forth in the Annual Investment Program. In instances where other priority projects are identified for implementation, a Sanggunian Bayan resolution should first be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sanggunian Panlalawigan.
19.5.5	We have recommended management to strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and utilization of the 20% DF to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes.
19.6.4	We have recommended management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer to the special account all amount allotted for the 20% development for easier monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.

20.1.5	We have recommended that enactment of a local ordinance creating the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Officer including the required staffing / personnel shall be pushed through. Upon enactment, consider the immediate hiring of qualified DRRM Officer and staff.
20.2.7	We have recommended that Vulnerability and Adaptation Assessment (VAA) shall be observed by the municipality so as to effectively formulate the Local Climate Change Action Plan (LCCAP). Such plan shall then address PPAs that will effectively manage the unavoidable impacts of climate change.
20.3.6	We have recommended that the LDRRMO prepare and submit LDRRMF accomplishment reports detailing the actual utilization of the fund on the prescribed time set forth by the COA Circular No. 2012-002.
20.4.8	We have recommended that the management treat the unexpended/ unobligated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for the current year and unexpended LDRRMF from prior years as Trust Liability and be duly transferred to a special trust fund account to avoid misuse of funds. We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to Financial Statements not only the unexpended balance from the current year but also from previous years.

21.3	We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sanggunian Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC as well as the CLJIP, if any. Moreover, we have recommended that management immediately submit the LCPC accomplishment reports to COA for verification and ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan be implemented as planned to attain the objectives of the fund. Henceforth, all disbursements should be supported with properly accomplished Obligation Requests.

22.6	We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly remit the taxes withheld pursuant to Section 4 of Revenue Regulations 6-2001. Also, the unremitted taxes amounting to ₱125,694.54 should be immediately remitted to the BIR.
22.7	Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and effect the following proposed adjusting entry to correct the balance of the Due to BIR account.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in the recording of transactions following the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular 2015-009. Moreover, to reflect the correct balances of the accounts and to present fairly the financial statements, we recommend that the proposed adjusting journal entries be prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made to correct the balances of the accounts
For compliance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015 should be utilized in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects. Likewise, the members of the Municipal Engineering familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH.
observations.managementAction: For compliance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings. Henceforth, observe the PS limitation in both budget preparation and budget execution phase pursuant to Section 325 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Will observe the PS limitation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly remit the taxes withheld as prescribed in Section 2.58 of Revenue Regulations 2-98. Also, the unremitted taxes amounting to ₱179,398.31 should be remitted immediately to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The taxes withheld from previous years were promptly remitted

The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert effort in determining the composition of unliquidated cash advances in the previous years
Demand letters to liquidate the cash advances be sent to the officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances
Henceforth, cash advances should only be granted to officers and employees with no records of unliquidated cash advances
Management demand from the retired MPDC the refund of payment of loan amortization amounting to ₱144,232.00. All retirement benefits due him should be withheld otherwise, this will become the personal liability of the person responsible for the remittance thereof.
Management stop the grant of cash advance to the municipal mayor for purposes other than travelling expenses.  Expenses of the municipal mayor should be paid following the standard process of a regular government disbursement.

Moreover, result of the inventory should be submitted to the audit team through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit a request for authority to write-off the receivables together with the necessary supporting document to the Regional Director, Commission on Audit for evaluation and possible dropping from the books of accounts. If said payables are not supported with documents to establish the claims, adjusting entries should be prepared.

Also, we have recommended that the Management exhausts all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish the correct amount of collectible for the year 2015 supported with certified list of taxpayers. The management should require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and submit to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in setting up the receivables and deferred income in order to present fairly the financial statements.

We also have recommended that the Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for government contracts are duly and properly supported before payment pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management stop utilizing the current year’s appropriations to pay prior year’s unbooked obligations and to strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations governing the expenditures within the limits of the current appropriations to avoid any issuance of suspensions or disallowances. 

We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all unpaid valid obligations accruing during the year are taken up in the books as payables.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all fund transfers to LGUs should be supported by Memorandum of Agreement and other necessary documents as provided in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001. Also, we have recommended that the management require the concerned barangay to immediately submit their liquidations for fund transfers granted from prior year.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management justify the noted the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management submit immediately to COA the copies of contract documents for the Bucloc Various Slope Protection. Henceforth, copies of duly perfected purchase orders/contracts should be submitted to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution thereof for prompt review and evaluation as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015 should be utilized in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer familiarize himself with the promulgations issued by the DPWH.
We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain ledger and property/stock cards as control records and to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records with the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

Management justify the above noted lapses and submit the missing documents for evaluation. 
The BAC should strictly follow the “pass/fail criteria in the conduct of bidding and automatically disqualify non-complying bidders.
The BAC create a Technical Working Group (TWG) which will assist in the procurement process.
Management should strictly comply with the regulations set forth under RA 9184, its implementing rules and regulations and promulgations by the GPPB.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted deficiencies, we have recommended that the bids and awards justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents for verification.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above mentioned audit observations, we have recommended that management justify the purchase of second hand instead of brand new vehicles as approved by the respective local sangguniang bayan as well as non-conduct of technical study before the purchase.

Moreover, we have recommended that management provide the technical and financial documents in support of the contract. Also, the official receipt of the first registration and the computation of Residual Useful Life of the vehicle should be submitted.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings. Henceforth, observe the PS limitation in both budget preparation and budget execution phase pursuant to Section 325 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality submit justification on the utilization of funds for other activities intended for the payment of current liabilities and implementation of programs under the LDRRMF, GAD and 20% development fund. We also have recommended that the municipality carefully monitor the cash balance in order to avoid incurrence of overdraft and prevent charges of technical malversation.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The management specifically identify and prepared a detail list of the priority projects to be funded under the 20% development fund which should include particular location and time frame of implementation. Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these programs or project should already been included in the planning and budgeting.
Submit the Program of Works as supporting documents to the Annual Investment Plan to aid in the further analysis of Appropriated Projects vis-a-vis actual expenditures. For lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained.
The management implement only projects set forth in the Annual Investment Program. In instances where other priority projects are identified for implementation, a Sangguniang Bayan resolution should be passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. 
The management require the MPDC to prepare and submit to the audit team the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund.
The management should strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental condition of constituents.
The management should require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all amounts allotted for the 20% development fund for easier monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, GAD Plan should be submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Office for review and to DILG for approval. Likewise, GAD plan and report should be accomplished using the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation:
1.10	We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of local road networks and the related existence and condition thereof, management should undertake the following:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of each year.

2.6	We have recommended that in order to establish accurately and reliably the balances of PPE accounts and forestall any losses, determine the actual existence and condition of properties, the Municipal Mayor form a committee which will oversee the actual inventory taking procedures and reconcile the report derived therein with the balance shown in the books of accounts. Moreover, result of the inventory should be submitted to the audit team through the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) for verification.

5.6	We have recommended that the management should determine the details of the recorded Land in the books. Also, we have recommended that the Management exhausts all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.
9.11	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit to the Commission on Audit all financial reports and accounts within the time prescribed by regulations.

10.11	We have recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume her previous position.
11.7	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.
12.7	We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of an approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2016 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

13.13	We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015 should be utilized in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects. Likewise, the members of the Municipal Engineering familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH.
Management Comment:
13.14	The matter was discussed with the concerned department and was informed of the noted lapses. The management will make sure to compute the total mark-up allowed for the infrastructure projects based on the Department Order of the DPWH.

16.3	In view of the noted violations, we have recommended the following:
The Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution properly authorizing the Municipality of Bucloc to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further the municipality should maintain a separate book for the said fund as well as establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers to account for its transactions; and
Submit the Special Allotment Release Orders and Notices of Authority to Debit Account Issued.

17.8	In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
The management specifically identify and prepared a detail list of the priority projects to be funded under the 20% development fund which should include particular location and time frame of implementation. Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these programs or project should already been included in the planning and budgeting.
Submit the Program of Works as supporting documents to the Annual Investment Plan to aid in the further analysis of Appropriated Projects vis-a-vis actual expenditures. For lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained.
The management should strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the preparation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental condition of constituents.
The management should require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all amounts allotted for the 20% development fund for easier monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.

18.7	In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that management:
Justify the noted lapses and henceforth, familiarize themselves with the relevant provisions with regard to the utilization of the LDRRM Funds;
Push for the enactment of a local ordinance creating the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Office including the required staffing/personnel. Upon enactment consider the immediate hiring of qualified DRRM Officer and staff;
Require the MDRRMO-Designate to prepare and submit to the audit team the report of utilization of the LDRRMF. Utilization reports should be submitted on the prescribed time set forth by the COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Submit the required supporting documents needed in order to validate whether the procured goods were distributed to its intended beneficiaries.
See to it that unexpended/unobligated funds from prior years LDRRM are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust fund account; and
Utilize the prior years’ trust liability for the acquisition of disaster preparedness equipment as well as immediate assistance and services to calamity stricken constituents.

19.8	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Also, submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC and Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2016, if any. Henceforth, observe and abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

21.9	In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor creates a GFPS Execom, TWG and Secretariat that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) as well as the required Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System;
The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adapt a Local GAD Code;
The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of HGDG and that the GAD plan are regularly updated; and
Henceforth, management sees to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the PPDO.
Management Comment:
21.10	The matter was already forwarded to the concerned office for future positive actions, creating a GFPS and GRMES. The Local GAD Code were already formulated and adapted for the succeeding year 2017. Since then, the MSWDO is the one in charge of identifying gender related issues, concern and activities for budgeting, but such will be done with the use of HGDG and will be further submitted for approval of the DILG and the PPDO.

We have recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer justify the payment of the transactions despite the absence of the BAC Resolution recommending the mode of procurement used and award of the contract
The Municipal Mayor justify entering into contract without the concurrence of the majority of the BAC
The management identify the method of procurement used and justify the non-conduct of competitive public bidding.
observations.managementAction: No action informed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractors to post warranty security for those which are still within the 1 year defect liability period. Henceforth, oblige all contractors to post the required warranty securities.
observations.managementAction: No action informed
No action informed

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its purchases and contracts to avoid imposition of sanctions by the BIR.
observations.managementAction: No action informed
